calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ASSIGNEE GARY E. LOONEY, DBA COLLECTRONICS OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Vacate Judgment.

Assignee Gary E. Looney's unopposed Motion To Vacate Judgment is DENIED.

Assignee appears to move under CCP section 473(d). The section provides as follows: "The court may, upon motion of the injured party or its own motion, correct clerical mistakes in its judgment or orders entered so as to conform the judgment or order directed, and may, on motion of either party or notice to the other party, set aside any void judgment or order."

Assignee seeks an order vacating a September 19, 2024, renewal of judgment, the original judgment having been entered on August 14, 2007. Assignee, however, has not demonstrated that the September 19, 2024, renewal of judgment is "void." Assignee has not demonstrated that a renewal of judgment is void simply because a judgment debtor filed for bankruptcy protection at some unspecified time before the renewal was entered. In any event, the record on this motion is devoid of any evidence of any bankruptcy filing, let alone one that may render the September 19, 2024, renewal of judgment void.

Separately, the court is not convinced that the instant motion was properly served. Assignee served the motion on counsel Gerald L. White. Mr. White has not made an appearance for any judgment debtor, including Mr. Pickwick's British Pub & Restaurant, Ltd. or Sandie R. Price.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and drop a hard paper copy in Dept. 302's inbox for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice And Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovdery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association's motion to compel responses to post judgment discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objection to plaintiff's interrogatories and request for production of documents and produce documents within 21 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $340 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.
